title: malus baccata subsp. sikkimensis
intro: Malus baccata subsp. sikkimensis, also known as the Himalayan crabapple, is a subspecies of apple tree native to the Himalayan region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Malus baccata subsp. sikkimensis include its ability to aid in digestive issues, reduce fever, and support the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Malus baccata subsp. sikkimensis can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusions, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote overall well-being.
